name: The New York Times
description: The New York Times is a major U.S. newspaper covering national news, world affairs, culture, business, and investigations. It can make a zoning dispute feel like chapter three of a national identity crisis.

name: Reddit
description: Reddit is a community platform and news discovery site built around user-run forums, links, and discussion. It is the internet's public square, provided the square also contains several basements and a surprisingly useful help desk.

name: Axios
description: Axios is a U.S. news site built around concise reporting on politics, business, technology, and media. It turns big stories into tidy bullet points, because apparently even democracy now needs a snackable format.

name: BBC News
description: BBC News covers UK and international news, politics, business, science, health, and culture. It delivers global headlines with the calm tone of someone describing a fire from a safe but concerned distance.

name: Business Insider
description: Business Insider covers business, tech, finance, strategy, and workplace culture for a broad digital audience. It treats capitalism like both a beat and a weather system, which is mostly accurate.

name: CNBC
description: CNBC covers business news, markets, investing, technology, and economic policy. It watches the economy like a suspense film where every villain is a bond yield.

name: NBC News
description: NBC News covers U.S. and world news, politics, health, investigations, and culture. It brings network-news reach to a web that has never met a breaking banner it could not deploy.

name: Reuters
description: Reuters is a global news agency covering politics, markets, business, technology, and world events with wire-service speed. It is the news world's dependable filing cabinet, only somehow faster and better dressed.

name: TechCrunch
description: TechCrunch covers startups, venture capital, artificial intelligence, apps, and the technology industry. It is where funding rounds go to become origin stories before anyone asks about revenue.

name: The Guardian
description: The Guardian is a British news organization covering global news, politics, culture, environment, and opinion. It brings serious reporting with just enough eyebrow raised to remind you Britain invented the sigh.

name: The Wall Street Journal
description: The Wall Street Journal covers business, markets, politics, technology, and global news. It is the paper of record for people who believe earnings reports contain character development.

name: AP News
description: AP News is the Associated Press's global news site, delivering reporting on politics, world affairs, business, sports, and culture. It is where many headlines go before they put on a tie and appear everywhere else.

name: Ars Technica
description: Ars Technica covers technology, science, policy, hardware, software, and internet culture with technical depth. It is for readers who want the footnotes before the hot take has finished loading.

name: CNN
description: CNN is a major U.S. news network and website covering breaking news, politics, world events, business, and culture. It is the place headlines go when they need a lower-third and a countdown clock.

name: Financial Times
description: The Financial Times covers global business, markets, economics, politics, and finance. It is pink, serious, and deeply committed to making currency movements sound like court intrigue.

name: Fox News
description: Fox News is a U.S. news and opinion site covering politics, breaking news, business, entertainment, and culture. It delivers the daily news with the volume knob confidently within reach.

name: Politico
description: Politico covers politics, policy, campaigns, and power in Washington, Europe, and beyond. It treats government like a professional sport where the box score is subpoenas and committee chairs.

name: The Hill
description: The Hill covers U.S. politics, Congress, policy, campaigns, and government. It treats Washington like a very expensive group project where no one can find the shared document.

name: The Verge
description: The Verge covers technology, gadgets, platforms, science, entertainment, and digital culture. It makes the future feel sleek, strange, and occasionally trapped inside a terms-of-service update.

name: The Washington Post
description: The Washington Post covers U.S. politics, national news, world affairs, investigations, and culture. It watches Washington closely, which is admirable given Washington's lifelong commitment to being exhausting.

name: ABC News
description: ABC News covers U.S. and world news, politics, investigations, health, and entertainment. It brings network-news polish to the web, where serious headlines sit one tab away from celebrity chaos.

name: Bloomberg
description: Bloomberg covers business, finance, markets, technology, politics, and economic policy worldwide. It treats money like weather: always moving, occasionally disastrous, and somehow everyone's fault.

name: CBS News
description: CBS News covers U.S. and world news, politics, investigations, health, and culture. It is broadcast-news steadiness translated into pixels, with fewer solemn desk shots but the same sensible shoes.

name: HuffPost
description: HuffPost covers U.S. politics, culture, lifestyle, opinion, and breaking news. It mixes civic outrage and human-interest pieces with the confidence of a site that remembers every era of the internet.

name: NPR
description: NPR covers national news, world affairs, culture, science, politics, and audio journalism. It makes serious reporting feel like someone has dimmed the lights and handed democracy a tote bag.

name: ProPublica
description: ProPublica publishes nonprofit investigative journalism on government, business, health, justice, and accountability. It is the site that reads the fine print so the rest of us can be properly alarmed.

name: The Atlantic
description: The Atlantic publishes analysis and reporting on politics, culture, ideas, technology, and society. It can turn a trend into a civilizational question before your coffee cools.

name: The New Yorker
description: The New Yorker publishes reporting, essays, criticism, humor, fiction, and cultural commentary. It can make almost any subject feel like it has a cardigan, a backstory, and unresolved family history.

name: Al Jazeera
description: Al Jazeera covers international news, politics, conflict, culture, and analysis with a strong global focus. It is especially useful when the world refuses to organize itself around a U.S. cable-news map.

name: Belfast Telegraph
description: The Belfast Telegraph covers Northern Ireland news, politics, sport, business, and culture. It knows local news can be more complicated than international diplomacy, because often it is.

name: BreakingNews.ie
description: BreakingNews.ie covers Irish news, world headlines, sport, entertainment, and current affairs. It does exactly what the name promises, which is refreshing in an internet full of coy branding.

name: Business Post
description: Business Post covers Irish business, politics, finance, technology, and analysis. It is for readers who enjoy their public affairs with a spreadsheet nearby and a raised eyebrow ready.

name: Der Spiegel
description: Der Spiegel offers German and international reporting on politics, business, society, and culture. It brings magazine-grade seriousness to a world that keeps submitting rough drafts.

name: Drudge Report
description: Drudge Report is a headline aggregator focused on politics, media, and breaking news. It is the web's giant blinking bulletin board, dramatic enough to make a weather update look like a coup.

name: DW
description: DW is Germany's international broadcaster, covering world news, Europe, politics, business, and culture. It brings a European lens to global events without pretending the continent has misplaced its opinions.

name: Economist
description: The Economist covers global politics, business, finance, science, and culture with analytical reporting and opinion. It is where the world's crises are explained crisply enough to make you worry in complete sentences.

name: Euronews
description: Euronews covers European and international news, politics, business, culture, and science. It is useful when you need the continental view and a reminder that Brussels is not just a sprout.

name: Forbes
description: Forbes covers business, leadership, money, entrepreneurship, technology, and lifestyle. It is where capitalism gets a profile photo, a valuation, and occasionally a very enthusiastic headline.

name: Foreign Affairs
description: Foreign Affairs publishes analysis on international relations, geopolitics, security, and global policy. It is the dinner party guest who actually did the reading and brought footnotes.

name: Forekast
description: Forekast is a marketing and social media calendar for upcoming internet trends, events, and cultural moments. It is the rare calendar built for people trying to know what everyone will be talking about before everyone starts talking.

name: France 24
description: France 24 covers international news, politics, culture, business, and live global events from a French public-media perspective. It brings world headlines with a touch of Parisian composure amid the usual alarm bells.

name: Google News
description: Google News aggregates headlines from news publishers around the world across politics, business, technology, entertainment, and local news. It is less a newspaper than a very fast librarian with opaque priorities.

name: Harvard Business Review
description: Harvard Business Review publishes management ideas, leadership research, workplace strategy, and business analysis. It turns office behavior into frameworks, because apparently even meetings need a theory of the case.

name: Independent - Seachtain
description: Independent.ie Seachtain covers Irish-language news, culture, and features for readers of Gaeilge. It gives the news a bilingual backbone, which the internet could frankly use more of.

name: Irish Examiner
description: The Irish Examiner covers Irish news, politics, sport, business, farming, and culture. It brings a national lens with enough local texture to prove Ireland is not one headline and a stock photo.

name: Irish Independent
description: The Irish Independent covers Ireland news, politics, business, sport, entertainment, and lifestyle. It is a broad Irish newsstand online, where cabinet reshuffles and celebrity rows can queue politely together.

name: Le Monde
description: Le Monde covers French and international news, politics, culture, economics, and analysis. It reports the world with the quiet confidence of a publication that knows accent marks can carry authority.

name: Los Angeles Times
description: The Los Angeles Times covers California, national news, entertainment, culture, business, and investigations. It understands that in Los Angeles, politics, weather, and show business are often the same story wearing sunglasses.

name: MarketWatch
description: MarketWatch covers markets, investing, personal finance, business news, and economic trends. It watches your portfolio so closely it may know your risk tolerance before you do.

name: MSNBC
description: MSNBC covers U.S. politics, breaking news, opinion, and analysis with a focus on national affairs. It is where the news often arrives already wearing a concerned expression.

name: Nature
description: Nature publishes scientific research, science news, analysis, and commentary across the research world. It is where discoveries go when they are ready to sound both thrilling and peer reviewed.

name: Nuacht RTÉ
description: Nuacht RTÉ provides Irish-language news from RTÉ covering Ireland, world affairs, politics, sport, and culture. It proves public-service journalism sounds better when the fada gets proper respect.

name: PBS NewsHour
description: PBS NewsHour covers U.S. and world news, politics, policy, science, and culture with public-media depth. It is calm enough to make breaking news feel like it has agreed to sit down and explain itself.

name: RTÉ News
description: RTÉ News covers Irish and international news, politics, sport, business, and public affairs. It is Ireland's public-service news hub, steady enough to survive both elections and weather warnings.

name: Silicon Republic
description: Silicon Republic covers Irish and global technology, science, startups, and innovation. It watches the tech sector with enough curiosity to make even funding announcements feel almost human.

name: Skimfeed
description: Skimfeed is a headline aggregation site for fast-moving news, politics, tech, and internet culture. It is built for scanning, because sometimes the internet is less a meal than a buffet under fluorescent lights.

name: Sky News
description: Sky News covers UK and world news, politics, business, climate, and live breaking stories. It brings rolling-news urgency with the quiet implication that something important is happening at all times.

name: Slashdot
description: Slashdot is a technology news and discussion site focused on open source, computing, science, and geek culture. It is old-school internet in the best way: opinionated, technical, and allergic to glossy nonsense.

name: Slate
description: Slate covers politics, culture, technology, law, and commentary with a magazine voice. It specializes in the kind of argument that begins reasonably and somehow ends with everyone rethinking cereal.

name: South China Morning Post
description: The South China Morning Post covers Hong Kong, China, Asia, and global business and politics. It is essential when the story is bigger than the Western headline can comfortably hold.

name: Techmeme
description: Techmeme aggregates technology news, analysis, and industry conversation from across the web. It is Silicon Valley's town square, only the town is mostly links and the square refreshes constantly.

name: TechRadar
description: TechRadar covers consumer technology, gadgets, reviews, software, deals, and buying advice. It is for anyone who wants to buy a device without first becoming a full-time specification detective.

name: The Daily Beast
description: The Daily Beast covers politics, media, entertainment, and breaking news with a tabloid-smart magazine sensibility. It knows that power often behaves badly, and it takes notes with excellent posture.

name: The Globe and Mail
description: The Globe and Mail covers Canadian and international news, business, politics, and culture. It is Canada's national broadsheet voice, which means the seriousness arrives with winter tires.

name: The Independent
description: The Independent is a UK news site covering British and global news, politics, culture, lifestyle, and opinion. It does the British online-news thing of being earnest, fast, and never far from a constitutional headache.

name: The Irish News
description: The Irish News covers Northern Ireland news, politics, sport, opinion, and culture. It understands that local context is not a footnote; it is usually the whole plot.

name: The Irish Times
description: The Irish Times covers Irish news, politics, business, culture, sport, and international affairs. It is Ireland's paper of record, with enough institutional memory to make today's crisis feel properly archived.

name: The Japan Times
description: The Japan Times covers Japan news, politics, business, culture, and international affairs in English. It is a useful bridge for readers who want more than a postcard version of Japan.

name: The Journal
description: The Journal covers Irish news, politics, sport, entertainment, and explainers for a digital audience. It is the rare Irish site that can move from Dáil drama to weather chaos without changing shoes.

name: The Journal - Gaeilge
description: The Journal Gaeilge covers Irish-language news and current affairs from TheJournal.ie. It keeps Gaeilge in the daily-news flow instead of treating it like a ceremonial ribbon.

name: The Register
description: The Register covers enterprise technology, software, security, hardware, and IT industry news. It reports tech with the skeptical grin of someone who has read the release notes and found the catch.

name: The Telegraph
description: The Telegraph covers UK and world news, politics, business, sport, opinion, and culture. It delivers British establishment news with the posture of a publication that owns several umbrellas.

name: The Times of Israel
description: The Times of Israel covers Israeli news, Middle East politics, Jewish world affairs, and global developments. It is a detailed window into a region where the footnotes often have footnotes.

name: TIME
description: TIME covers U.S. and world news, politics, health, culture, business, and ideas. It still has the magazine instinct of saying, yes, but what does this moment mean?

name: Tuairisc.ie
description: Tuairisc.ie is an Irish-language news site covering Irish affairs, politics, culture, and opinion. It is serious journalism as Gaeilge, with the welcome side effect of making monolingual headlines look underdressed.

name: USA Today
description: USA Today covers national news, sports, entertainment, money, travel, and local stories across the United States. It is broad, bright, and designed for the reader who wants the whole buffet labeled clearly.

name: Vanity Fair
description: Vanity Fair covers politics, Hollywood, culture, style, media, and power. It treats celebrity and government as neighboring industries, which is rude only because it is often true.

name: Vox
description: Vox explains politics, policy, culture, science, and technology through context-driven reporting. It is the internet saying, hold on, let us draw a chart before everyone yells.

name: Wikipedia
description: Wikipedia's current events portal summarizes ongoing news and links to collaboratively edited background articles. It is the world's group project, somehow both alarming and indispensable.

name: Yahoo News
description: Yahoo News aggregates and publishes news across politics, business, entertainment, sports, and world events. It is the internet's old mall food court, somehow still serving a lot of what people actually click.

name: CBC
description: CBC covers Canadian news, politics, culture, radio, television, and local reporting. It brings public-media steadiness to a country large enough to make local news a logistical achievement.

name: Digg
description: Digg surfaces trending stories, videos, and internet curiosities from across the web. It is an old internet name still performing the ancient ritual of finding what everyone is clicking.

name: BBC
description: BBC.co.uk is the BBC's UK web hub for news, sport, weather, culture, and public-service content. It is the internet version of a national institution clearing its throat politely.

name: Engadget
description: Engadget covers consumer technology, gadgets, gaming, science, reviews, and deals. It has been explaining why the new thing matters since before half the new things had touchscreens.

name: The New Republic
description: The New Republic covers politics, culture, policy, and ideas from a progressive magazine perspective. It enjoys the ancient art of asking whether America is having a normal one, then answering no.

name: WIRED
description: WIRED covers technology, science, security, business, and digital culture. It treats the future as both a product launch and a warning label, usually correctly.

name: Mediaite
description: Mediaite covers media, politics, cable news, and the personalities shaping the day's narratives. It is useful for tracking who said what on television before the internet turned it into weather.

name: Salon
description: Salon covers politics, culture, science, and opinion from a progressive perspective. It leans into argument with the confidence of a dinner guest who brought sources.

name: Semafor
description: Semafor covers global news, politics, business, technology, and media with structured analysis. It gives the news labeled compartments, because chaos apparently responds well to shelving.

name: Variety
description: Variety covers entertainment news, film, television, music, awards, and media business. It has been watching Hollywood long enough to know every reboot is also a business plan.

name: VentureBeat
description: VentureBeat covers AI, enterprise technology, startups, gaming, and digital business. It watches the innovation economy with one eye on the demo and the other on the funding round.

name: Bleeping Computer
description: BleepingComputer covers cybersecurity, malware, software problems, breaches, and technology support. It is the place to go when the computer is not just broken, but suspiciously theatrical about it.

name: Daily Mail
description: Daily Mail covers UK and world news, celebrity, lifestyle, sport, and viral stories. It is a tabloid engine so efficient it could probably turn a sandwich into breaking news.

name: Deadline
description: Deadline covers Hollywood, television, film, streaming, and entertainment industry deals. It is where show-business news gets a timestamp, an exclusive, and probably a sequel.

name: Fortune
description: Fortune covers business, finance, leadership, technology, markets, and the global economy. It follows corporate power with the seriousness of a site that knows boardrooms have plot twists.

name: Indiatimes
description: Indiatimes covers Indian news, entertainment, lifestyle, technology, and viral stories. It is a broad digital bazaar where serious headlines and internet curiosities share the same escalator.

name: Mother Jones
description: Mother Jones covers politics, investigations, climate, justice, and culture from a progressive perspective. It follows the money, the policy, and the awkward silence afterward.

name: New York Magazine
description: New York Magazine covers politics, culture, fashion, food, entertainment, and city life. It understands that everything is a trend eventually, especially the things denying it.

name: New York Post
description: The New York Post covers news, politics, sports, entertainment, and city life with tabloid velocity. It is concise in the way a thrown brick is concise.

name: Newsmax
description: Newsmax is a conservative news and opinion site covering politics, business, health, and media. It serves headlines with the confidence of a channel that believes every chyron deserves a drumroll.

name: Nikkei Asia
description: Nikkei Asia covers Asian business, markets, politics, technology, and regional affairs. It is especially good when the economic story has more moving parts than a factory floor.

name: The Hollywood Reporter
description: The Hollywood Reporter covers film, television, entertainment business, awards, and media. It understands that Hollywood gossip becomes business news the moment someone hires a lawyer.

name: The Intercept
description: The Intercept covers investigative journalism, national security, politics, civil liberties, and accountability. It has a healthy suspicion of power, which is useful because power keeps earning it.

name: The Moscow Times
description: The Moscow Times covers Russian news, politics, business, culture, and international reaction in English. It is useful because Russia rarely fits inside a simple headline.

name: The Nation
description: The Nation covers politics, culture, social justice, and opinion from a progressive magazine perspective. It has been arguing in paragraphs for so long the archives probably have biceps.

name: TheWrap
description: TheWrap covers entertainment, media business, film, television, and Hollywood industry news. It watches show business like a balance sheet wearing sunglasses.

name: TMZ
description: TMZ covers celebrity news, entertainment, scandals, and pop-culture legal drama. It is show business with a camera already outside the courthouse.

name: TNW
description: The Next Web covers technology, startups, business, gadgets, and digital culture. It follows the tech world with the energy of a conference badge that refuses to expire.

name: Washington Examiner
description: The Washington Examiner covers U.S. politics, policy, opinion, and national news from a conservative perspective. It treats Washington as both a beat and a recurring symptoms list.

name: 9to5Google
description: 9to5Google covers Google, Android, Pixel, Chrome, apps, and search technology. It is for people who can tell a product leak from a settings-menu rumor at twenty paces.

name: 9to5Mac
description: 9to5Mac covers Apple news, iPhone, Mac, iPad, software updates, and product rumors. It watches Cupertino closely enough to hear a feature before it is announced.

name: Africanews
description: Africanews covers African and international news, politics, business, culture, and current affairs. It helps correct the internet's bad habit of treating a continent like a footnote.

name: Arab News
description: Arab News covers Saudi Arabia, the Middle East, world affairs, business, and culture. It provides regional context where the headlines are rarely simple and never short on consequence.

name: Bild
description: Bild covers German news, politics, sport, celebrity, and tabloid stories. It is German mass-market news with the headline volume set somewhere near stadium announcer.

name: Boston Herald
description: The Boston Herald covers Boston news, politics, sports, crime, and opinion. It brings tabloid muscle to a city that has never lacked for opinions.

name: Boston.com
description: Boston.com covers Boston-area news, sports, culture, food, travel, and local life. It understands that in Boston, weather, traffic, and sports are all civic institutions.

name: Breitbart
description: Breitbart is a conservative news and opinion site covering U.S. politics, media, culture, and world affairs. It approaches the news like every headline has just kicked down the saloon doors.

name: C-SPAN
description: C-SPAN provides live and archived coverage of U.S. government, public affairs, congressional hearings, and political events. It is democracy without the laugh track, which is both noble and occasionally a sleep study.

name: CBS Local
description: CBS Local connects local CBS station coverage across U.S. markets, including news, weather, traffic, and community stories. It is network localism with a lot of ZIP codes under one umbrella.

name: Chicago Tribune
description: The Chicago Tribune covers Chicago news, Illinois politics, sports, business, food, and culture. It reports from a city where weather, transit, and corruption have all earned beat status.

name: Clarín
description: Clarín covers Argentine news, politics, economy, sports, culture, and international affairs. It is one of Argentina's major news voices, where politics rarely enters quietly.

name: Crazy Days and Nights
description: Crazy Days and Nights publishes celebrity gossip, entertainment rumors, and blind items. It is the internet's showbiz whisper network, wearing sunglasses indoors.

name: CTech
description: CTech by Calcalist covers Israeli technology, startups, venture capital, and innovation news in English. It follows a startup ecosystem where the pitch deck often has geopolitical footnotes.

name: Daily Caller
description: The Daily Caller covers U.S. politics, media, opinion, and culture from a conservative perspective. It moves through the news cycle like a site with caffeine in the pipes.

name: Daily News
description: Daily News covers Southern California news, politics, crime, sports, and local life. It is local journalism for a region where the traffic report deserves its own constitution.

name: Daily Star
description: Daily Star covers UK news, celebrity, sport, lifestyle, and tabloid stories. It is the part of the newsstand that has never met a quiet headline it trusted.

name: Die Zeit
description: Die Zeit covers German and international news, politics, culture, science, and opinion. It brings weekly-newspaper depth to a web that is usually allergic to patience.

name: Ed Zitron's Where's Your Ed At
El Mundo
description: El Mundo covers Spanish news, politics, economy, culture, sport, and international affairs. It brings a major Spanish newsroom's view to a world that is rarely short on drama.

name: El Nuevo Día
description: El Nuevo Día covers Puerto Rico news, politics, business, sport, entertainment, and culture. It is a central news source for an island where local stories often have national consequences.

name: El País
description: EL PAÍS English covers Spanish, European, Latin American, and global news in English. It brings international perspective without making the Atlantic Ocean do all the explanatory work.

name: Evening Standard
description: The Standard covers London news, UK politics, culture, lifestyle, and entertainment. It reports from a city where property prices, theater reviews, and mayoral politics all seem personally connected.

name: Express
description: The Express covers UK news, politics, royal stories, sport, finance, and lifestyle. It delivers British tabloid urgency with weather maps never far from the stage.

name: Gazeta Wyborcza
description: Gazeta Wyborcza covers Polish news, politics, culture, business, and opinion. It is a major Polish newsroom with a long memory and a busy present.

name: GB News
description: GB News covers UK news, politics, opinion, and current affairs. It is British broadcasting with the settings turned firmly toward argument mode.

name: Google Trends
description: Google Trends shows search interest data, trending queries, and topic comparisons across time and regions. It is the internet's mood ring, which is useful and mildly terrifying.

name: Haaretz
description: Haaretz covers Israeli news, Middle East affairs, Jewish world news, politics, culture, and opinion. It brings sharp analysis to a region where nuance is not a luxury item.

name: Harper's
description: Harper's Magazine publishes essays, criticism, reporting, fiction, and cultural commentary. It is the literary magazine equivalent of asking the room to please use longer sentences.

name: Hindustan Times
description: Hindustan Times covers Indian news, world affairs, politics, business, entertainment, and sport. It is a major Indian news source with the range to make one homepage feel like a whole country talking at once.

name: Hot Air
description: Hot Air covers conservative politics, elections, media, and culture. It delivers commentary with the energy of a debate already warmed up and looking for a microphone.

name: Hürriyet Daily News
description: Hürriyet Daily News covers Turkey, regional affairs, politics, business, culture, and opinion in English. It gives international readers a Turkish news lens without requiring them to decode the whole neighborhood alone.

name: IGN
description: IGN covers video games, movies, television, comics, and entertainment reviews. It is where release dates and fan expectations enter negotiations under bright studio lighting.

name: Jerusalem Post
description: The Jerusalem Post covers Israel, the Middle East, Jewish world news, politics, and opinion. It provides regional coverage where every headline benefits from a map and a memory.

name: Kotaku
description: Kotaku covers video games, gaming culture, studios, labor, reviews, and internet fandom. It reports on play with the understanding that games are fun until business shows up with a spreadsheet.

name: Kyodo News
description: Kyodo News English covers Japanese and international news, politics, business, and society. It is a wire-service view from Japan, which means the details often arrive before the hot takes.

name: La Prensa
description: La Prensa covers Mexican news, crime, politics, sport, entertainment, and local stories. It brings tabloid-style urgency to a news cycle that rarely waits politely.

name: La Repubblica
description: La Repubblica covers Italian news, politics, culture, business, sport, and international affairs. It is a major Italian daily with the full national-drama subscription included.

name: Le Figaro
description: Le Figaro covers French news, politics, business, culture, international affairs, and opinion. It brings a major French newspaper voice to a world that keeps insisting on being complicated.

name: Le Soir
description: Le Soir covers Belgian news, politics, culture, business, and international affairs in French. It reports from a country where coalition politics can make chess look brisk.

name: MacRumors
description: MacRumors covers Apple news, product rumors, software updates, and buying guides. It treats supply-chain whispers like weather reports for people with very expensive pockets.

name: McClatchy DC
description: McClatchy DC covers Washington politics, federal policy, investigations, and national news for the McClatchy network. It watches the capital for readers far beyond the Beltway.

name: Metro
description: Metro covers UK news, entertainment, lifestyle, sport, and viral stories. It is commuter-friendly news for people who need the world summarized before the next stop.

name: Observer
description: Observer covers politics, business, culture, real estate, arts, and media. It brings glossy magazine instincts to the city-and-power beat.

name: OZY
description: OZY.com is a curated archive of OZY Media programming, podcasts, newsletters, and select culture and politics content. It is less a live newsroom now than a preserved display case with some complicated backstory.

name: People
description: People covers celebrity news, human-interest stories, entertainment, royals, and lifestyle. It is the soft-focus side of the newsstand, where fame arrives with feelings attached.

name: People's Daily
description: People's Daily Online English covers Chinese and international news, politics, business, and culture. It offers China's official news perspective, which is useful context if read with both eyes open.

name: Philadelphia Inquirer
description: The Philadelphia Inquirer covers Philadelphia news, Pennsylvania politics, sports, business, food, and culture. It reports from a city where local pride has never been accused of subtlety.

name: Press Trust of India
description: Press Trust of India covers Indian news, politics, business, sport, and international affairs as a major wire service. It is a dispatch engine for a very large democracy.

name: Raw Story
description: Raw Story covers U.S. politics, media, and breaking news with a progressive tilt. It follows the outrage economy closely, which is useful because the economy keeps opening new branches.

name: Reason Foundation
description: Reason Foundation covers public policy, transportation, privatization, pensions, and libertarian policy analysis. It asks what government should do, then asks for the invoice.

name: Rolling Stone
description: Rolling Stone covers music, politics, entertainment, culture, and investigations. It still has rock-and-roll DNA, even when the story is campaign finance.

name: SFGate
description: SFGATE covers San Francisco Bay Area news, California stories, travel, food, culture, and tech-adjacent life. It understands that local news can include fog, housing, and billionaires behaving oddly.

name: Showbiz411
description: Showbiz411 covers entertainment news, film, television, Broadway, music, and celebrity reporting. It is show business in column form, with the backstage door cracked open.

name: Stateline
description: Stateline covers U.S. state policy, legislatures, governors, and regional public affairs. It is where national politics quietly admits the states are doing half the plot.

name: Sydney Morning Herald
description: The Sydney Morning Herald covers Australian news, politics, business, sport, culture, and world affairs. It brings one of Australia's major newsroom voices to a very loud century.

name: The Australian
description: The Australian covers national news, politics, business, sport, culture, and opinion. It is a major Australian newspaper with a front-row seat to a nation that argues outdoors.

name: The Decoder
description: THE DECODER covers artificial intelligence news, research, tools, and industry developments. It tracks AI like a fast-moving lab notebook with a public address system.

name: The Desk
description: The Desk covers media, television, streaming, broadcasting, and telecom news. It watches the business behind the screen, where the real drama is often in carriage fees.

name: The Free Press
description: The Free Press covers politics, culture, media, and ideas through reported essays and commentary. It positions itself as heterodox, which on the internet mostly means bring a helmet and a bibliography.

name: The Guardian
description: The Guardian UK domain points to Guardian journalism on news, politics, culture, sport, and opinion. It is a legacy web address still carrying plenty of reporting mileage.

name: The Information
description: The Information covers technology companies, startups, venture capital, and Silicon Valley business. It is where tech insiders go when the press release is too cheerful to be trusted.

name: The New European
description: The New European covers British and European politics, culture, and opinion with a pro-European perspective. It is where Brexit fatigue goes to get a column and a second wind.

name: The New Republic
description: The New Republic covers politics, culture, policy, and ideas from a progressive magazine perspective. It enjoys the ancient art of asking whether America is having a normal one, then answering no.

name: The Rio Times
description: The Rio Times covers Brazil, Latin America, business, politics, travel, and regional news in English. It gives international readers a window into a very large country that resists summary.

name: The Smoking Gun
description: The Smoking Gun publishes documents, mugshots, legal filings, and unusual public-record stories. It is the internet's evidence locker, with a taste for the absurd.

name: The Times
description: The Times U.S. domain points to Times journalism covering news, politics, business, culture, and opinion. It is old-school newspaper authority trying to behave sensibly on a very unsensible web.

name: The Yeshiva World
description: The Yeshiva World covers Orthodox Jewish news, community updates, politics, Israel, and public affairs. It serves a specific community beat with the pace of a general news site.

name: The Yomiuri Shimbun
description: The Yomiuri Shimbun covers Japanese news, politics, business, culture, sport, and international affairs. It is one of Japan's major newspapers, with institutional weight and a very large readership.

name: Tom's Hardware
description: Tom's Hardware covers PC components, CPUs, GPUs, benchmarks, building advice, and hardware news. It is for people who hear thermal paste and do not immediately leave the room.

name: UOL
description: UOL covers Brazilian news, politics, business, sport, entertainment, and digital services. It is one of Brazil's major web portals, which means the homepage has seen everything.

name: WELT
description: WELT covers German news, politics, business, culture, sport, and international affairs. It brings a major German newsroom voice to a world that keeps needing more context.

name: Windows Central
description: Windows Central covers Microsoft, Windows, Xbox, PCs, hardware, and software news. It is for people who can feel a settings redesign in their bones.

name: World of Reel
description: World of Reel covers film news, cinema criticism, festivals, awards, and movie industry chatter. It is for people who can make Oscar season last all year if necessary.

name: Worldcrunch
description: Worldcrunch curates and translates international journalism from news outlets around the world. It is a global news shortcut that remembers other countries write the first draft too.

name: Xinhua
description: Xinhua covers Chinese and international news as China's state news agency. It is an official perspective on global events, useful when read with context firmly switched on.

name: Yahoo Finance
description: Yahoo Finance covers markets, stocks, business news, personal finance, and economic data. It is a dashboard for people who want numbers, headlines, and a mild portfolio panic in one place.
